explanation: This LTL property states that the robot must continually monitor its health and performance (monitorHealth). If the robot's health falls below a threshold (health_lessThan_threshold), or if it determines it cannot complete the task (taskCannotComplete), the robot should then explain why (provideReason). The `G` operator ensures this happens globally, and the implication `->` ensures the explanation only happens when a condition is met. The `G` is used to check for a condition and the implication means, if health is less than threshold or task cannot complete, then there should be an explanation. The health check is the main task that is constantly monitored.

The robot continually monitors its health and performance. If at any point, the robot determines its health is below an acceptable level or the task cannot be completed, it provides a reason/explanation.

This LTL property states that it is always the case (G) that if the robot is continually monitoring its health and performance (monitorHealth), and if it is assessing whether it is able to undertake the task (assessment), and if the robot determines that the task cannot be completed (taskCannotComplete), then it should always provide a reason (provideReason) for not undertaking the task.

The LTL property states that globally, if the robot is monitoring its health, and the robot's health is below a defined threshold, and the task cannot be completed, then the robot should provide a reason for why the task cannot be completed. This reflects the prompt's requirements for the robot to assess its health and performance, determine if it can do the task, and explain why not if it cannot.

This LTL property states that the robot must continually monitor its health and performance and assess its ability to undertake the task. If at any point the robot's health is below an acceptable level (health_lessThan_threshold), and the robot assesses that it cannot complete the task (taskCannotComplete), then the robot must explain why the task cannot be completed (provideReason) and if detailed explanations are requested it must be detailed (detailRequested). The property combines a global constraint with a conditional action, ensuring that the robot provides explanations under the specified conditions.

This LTL property states that the robot must continually monitor its health and assess task suitability. If it determines the task is not completable (taskCannotComplete), it must explain why (provideReason), always.

The LTL property states that the robot should always monitor its health. If the robot's health is below a defined acceptable level it should calculate the likelihood of successfully finishing the assigned task.  If the robot determines it is unable to complete the task (taskCannotComplete), it should explain why not, specifically its health status and also provide a reason for this decision. This must hold true at all times.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot is monitoring its health and the health is below a defined threshold and there is an assessment occurring, and the robot's health is below the threshold, then the robot must, in the next state, provide a reason for not being able to undertake the task.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot is monitoring its health and if it is performing an assessment, and if the task cannot be completed, then it will provide a reason. N_DURATION is the time duration for this requirement.

This LTL formula states that, globally, if the robot is monitoring its health and performing assessments (`monitorHealth & assessment`), and if the robot determines it is unable to complete the task (`taskCannotComplete`), then in the next state, the robot provides a reason for not being able to complete the task (`X provideReason`).

This LTL property states that the robot continually monitors its health and the ability to complete the task. If at any point, the robot's health is below an acceptable level or it determines the task cannot be completed, the robot must then provide an explanation for why it cannot complete the task. The 'G' operator ensures this behavior is maintained globally.  The property can be understood as:  Globally, if the robot is monitoring its health and/or is evaluating the task, and task completion is impossible or the robots health is below a threshold, then the robot will provide an explanation.

The LTL property states that it is always the case that if the robot is monitoring its health and performing assessments, and the task cannot be completed, then the robot should explain why the task is unable to be completed. This ensures that the robot provides a reason for its inability to undertake the task, adhering to the requirement.

This LTL formula states that globally, the robot is continuously monitoring its health. If the robot's health falls below a threshold, and the task cannot be completed, then the robot must explain why not. This captures the essence of the prompt: continual assessment, identifying inability to undertake the task, and providing an explanation.

This LTL property states that the robot should always monitor its health and performance. If the robot assesses that it is unable to undertake the task (e.g., due to health issues), then it must explain why within a fixed amount of time (N_DURATION).

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot is monitoring its health and the assessment indicates a potential issue (health below a threshold), and the robot decides the task cannot be completed, it should globally provide a reason. We use 'G' (Globally) to represent this continual assessment and response requirement. 'bool_exp1' captures the monitoring and assessment, 'bool_exp2' signifies that the task can not be completed and 'bool_exp3' says the robot should provide a reason for this

This LTL property ensures that the robot continually monitors its health. If at any point the robot's health falls below an acceptable level and it assesses that it is unable to undertake the task, it must provide an explanation. This explanation must also occur if the robot has determined that the task cannot be completed, and the robot must provide a reason for this determination.

The LTL formula states that globally (always) if the robot is continually monitoring its health and assessing its task suitability, and if it then decides that the task cannot be completed, it must, at the next time step, provide an explanation for why the task cannot be completed. N_DURATION sets a time bound for the explanation.  This accurately reflects the natural language requirement.
This LTL property states that globally, if the robot is monitoring its health and the robot's health is below a defined acceptable level, then it should provide a reason.

This LTL property states that the robot must continually monitor its health.  If, at any point, the robot's health is below a certain threshold (health_lessThan_threshold) and the task cannot be completed (taskCannotComplete), the robot must provide a reason (provideReason) for the task's inability. N_DURATION is a constant that defines the time window for the guarantee.

This LTL property states that the robot should continuously monitor its health and performance (`monitorHealth`). If the robot determines that it is unable to undertake the task (`taskCannotComplete`), then it must provide a reason (`provideReason`) within a specified duration (N_DURATION). The structure is: Globally (G), if health check is on and task cannot be completed, within a duration the robot provides a reason.

The LTL formula ensures that the robot continually monitors its health and if the health is below a threshold (health_lessThan_threshold) and also if it decides that task cannot complete (taskCannotComplete) , it should provide a reason (provideReason) within a certain time frame, and then it should explain why it believes it's suitable for the mission(explainSuitable).
The robot should always monitor its health and performance. If its health is less than a threshold or it cannot complete the task, it should explain why it cannot.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot is monitoring its health and making a decision, and the robot's health is below a defined acceptable level, or if the robot determines the task cannot be completed, then in the next state, the robot must provide a reason for not being able to undertake the task.

The robot should always monitor its health (monitorHealth). If the robot's health is below a defined threshold (health_lessThan_threshold), and if the task cannot be completed (taskCannotComplete), then the robot must provide an explanation (explain). This explanation should detail why the task cannot be completed (provideReason).

This LTL formula states that globally, the robot is continually monitoring its health and the ability to undertake the task. If at any point the robot's health is below an acceptable level or the task cannot be completed, the robot must provide a reason (explain why not) for its inability to undertake the task.

The LTL property states that globally, if the robot is monitoring its health, assessing its ability to undertake a task, and detects the robot's health is below an acceptable level or is unable to complete the task, then the robot should provide a reason.

The LTL property ensures that the robot continuously assesses its health and performance. If at any point the robot's health falls below an acceptable threshold or the robot determines the task cannot be completed (health_lessThan_threshold | taskCannotComplete), it should provide a reason (provideReason). This provides both immediate and continual monitoring.

The LTL property states that globally, if the robot is monitoring its health, it will eventually assess whether it can undertake the task. If the robot determines it cannot complete the task (taskCannotComplete), it will eventually explain why (provideReason).  N_DURATION represents a time bound for the eventualities.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot is monitoring its health (monitorHealth), and it is performing an assessment (assessment), then it should provide a reason (provideReason).  It ensures that whenever a health check and assessment happen, the robot provides a reason, capturing the requirement to explain why it is unable to undertake the task.
The robot should continually monitor its health and assess if it can undertake the task. If the robot determines it's unable, it must provide an explanation.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot is monitoring its health and is also assessing the situation, and if it decides the task cannot be completed (taskCannotComplete), then it must provide a reason (provideReason) within a certain time frame (N_DURATION).

This LTL property states that the robot, when in a state of assessment and health monitoring (G(monitorHealth & assessment)), should, if it determines it cannot complete the task (taskCannotComplete), explain why (explain & provideReason).  The 'N_DURATION' parameter is not used in this template.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot is monitoring its health and making decisions (assessment), then if the robot's health is less than the threshold, then the robot should provide a reason.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot is monitoring its health and undertaking a mission, and the task cannot be completed, then the robot must explain why not. The `N_DURATION` value implies that within some bounded time frame (e.g., a few time steps), the explanation must be provided. This captures the core requirement of the natural language prompt.

This LTL property states that the robot must continually monitor its health and performance (monitorHealth & assessment) and assess if it can undertake the task. If the robot determines that it is not able to perform the task (taskCannotComplete), then it must provide a reason why (provideReason) in the next state (X). The "G" operator ensures this holds globally.

This LTL property states that the robot, if monitoring its health, must continually assess its health and performance. If the robot's health falls below a threshold, it should explain its health status and the reason it cannot undertake the task. The task completion status is unavailable.

This LTL property specifies that the robot should continually monitor its health and performance (monitorHealth). If at any point the robot assesses that it cannot undertake the task (taskCannotComplete), it must provide an explanation (explain) regarding why it is unable to complete the task.

The robot should continually monitor its health and performance. If the robot's health is below a threshold and the robot determines it cannot undertake the task (taskCannotComplete), it should explain (explain) why (provideReason) this is the case. This is captured by the G (always) operator applied to the implication.  The implication states that if the robot's health is below threshold (health_lessThan_threshold) then if task cannot be completed (taskCannotComplete) it must explain why (explain & provideReason).

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot is monitoring its health and assessing its ability to undertake the task (implied by 'monitorHealth' and 'assessment'), and it subsequently decides the task cannot be completed ('taskCannotComplete'), it should provide an explanation ('provideReason') within a certain time frame (N_DURATION).

This LTL property ensures that if the robot is monitoring its health (`monitorHealth`), it should continually assess its ability to undertake the task (`assessment`).  If the robot determines it cannot complete the task (`taskCannotComplete`), then it must provide a reason (`provideReason`).  The property uses template 10, which is G (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3), meaning that the implication must always be true.  bool_exp2 is set to assess & monitorHealth, and bool_exp3 is taskCannotComplete -> provideReason.

This LTL property states that the robot must continuously monitor its health and assess task feasibility. If the robot determines it cannot complete the task (taskCannotComplete), it must provide a detailed explanation (detailed) of the reason, starting immediately (at time 0) and continuing for the next N_DURATION time steps. The property uses a 'G (G[0,N_DURATION] ...)' pattern to ensure continual checking and explanation. The `monitorHealth & calculateTaskCompletionProbability` part makes sure the robot assesses its health and the task's completion probability.

This LTL property states that the robot should always (globally, G) be monitoring its health and performance (monitorHealth). If the robot's health is below an acceptable level (health_lessThan_threshold), the robot must provide an explanation (explain), and if the robot's health is not below the threshold (!health_lessThan_threshold), the requirement must be satisfied.

The LTL formula ensures that the robot continually monitors its health status. If the robot's health drops below a threshold (health_lessThan_threshold), the formula states it should then assess the mission's feasibility (calculateTaskCompletionProbability).  If the robot determines it cannot complete the task (taskCannotComplete), it should provide an explanation (explain) of why the task cannot be completed.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the robot is monitoring its health and making a decision, and the robot's health is less than a defined threshold, and the task cannot be completed, then the robot should explain why the task cannot be completed or it will explain the health status. N_DURATION is a time bound on how long the explanation must occur.
The LTL property states that the robot must continually monitor its health and ability to undertake the task. If the robot determines it cannot undertake the task, it must provide an explanation.

This LTL property states that it is always the case (globally) that if the robot is monitoring its health and performance and is in a state of assessment and the robot's health is below an acceptable threshold and the task cannot be completed, then, at the next time step, the robot will explain why the task cannot be completed.  This captures the essence of the prompt.

The LTL property states that it is always the case that if the robot is monitoring its health and the robot's health is below an acceptable level, and the task cannot be completed, the robot must provide a reason for why the task can't be completed over a certain duration (N_DURATION).

This LTL property ensures that if the robot is monitoring its health and the robot's health is below an acceptable level, then the robot will provide a reason or justification (provideReason) within a finite duration.  The property states: Globally, if both the monitorHealth and health_lessThan_threshold conditions are true, then, within N_DURATION time steps, provideReason must be true.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the robot is monitoring its health and assessing its situation, and also determines the task cannot be completed, then the robot will always provide an explanation of why it cannot complete the task.

This LTL property states that globally, if the robot is monitoring its health and the assessment is being performed, and if the health is below a threshold, then the robot should provide a reason for not undertaking the task. The robot must continually assess its health and performance and provide a reason if it is not able to undertake the task.
